Name: dynamic_precision_optimization
Title: Dynamic Precision Optimization with Runtime Profiling for Efficient Inference
Short Hypothesis: By employing runtime profiling to dynamically allocate numerical precision for inference tasks, we can achieve significant latency reduction and resource efficiency without introducing the overhead of complex decision mechanisms.
Related Work: Current literature, such as Dynamic Kernel Selection and mixed-precision inference methods, often rely on static or heuristic approaches that do not adapt well to real-time workload variances. Our proposal distinguishes itself by integrating runtime profiling metrics to inform precision allocation, directly linking empirical throughput gains to hardware characteristics.
Abstract: In this proposal, we introduce a novel framework for Dynamic Precision Optimization (DPO) that utilizes runtime profiling to adjust numerical precision during inference on GPUs. Our approach aims to enhance latency and throughput by dynamically allocating precision based on the observed execution conditions of various workloads. By profiling key metrics such as occupancy, memory bandwidth utilization, and arithmetic intensity, DPO establishes a direct correlation between these factors and optimal precision levels for each inference task. We hypothesize that this method will yield substantial improvements in inference speed without compromising accuracy. Experimental validation will involve benchmarking against existing static precision methods, measuring p50/p99 latency, throughput, and energy efficiency across a range of models. Our findings are expected to provide a clear empirical basis for precision allocation strategies, contributing to the ongoing discourse on efficient GPU utilization in machine learning applications.
Experiments: 1. Implement a baseline static precision allocation strategy for common models (e.g., ResNet50, BERT).
2. Develop the DPO framework that collects runtime profiling metrics during inference.
3. Conduct A/B testing by comparing the static precision method against the DPO framework, measuring latency, throughput, and energy consumption.
4. Analyze the profiling data to identify correlations between performance metrics and optimal precision levels, then assess improvements in inference speed and resource utilization.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential risks include variations in performance gains depending on the specific model architectures and workloads. Limitations may arise if the profiling mechanisms introduce non-negligible overhead or if the gains do not sufficiently outweigh the costs of dynamic adjustments.
